perturbation.original_text: 3.4) At the conclusion of the Evaluation Petro-Lube shall either return promptly to (Your Company Name) or it's Assigns, or at (Your Company Name)'s option, destroy any remaining Sample in whatever form then in its possession or control and verify such to (Your Company Name).
perturbation.changed_text: 3.4) At the conclusion of the Evaluation, Petro-Lube may retain a portion of the remaining Sample for internal research or quality control purposes, with the remainder to either be returned promptly to (Your Company Name) or it's Assigns, or at (Your Company Name)'s option, destroyed. Verification of destruction is not required.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates prompt return or destruction of *any* remaining sample. The change allows Petro-Lube to retain a portion and removes the verification requirement for destruction. This creates an internal contradiction if Section 4 outlines strict Non-Disclosure of Confidential Information, including data derived from the samples.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1) It shall not be necessary for either Party to disclose to the other Party any confidential technical or commercial data or information about its products, technology, processes, commercial activities or otherwise for the purposes of this Agreement. To the extent that either Party does disclose such data or information to the other Party, the other Party shall be entitled to regard such information as non-confidential in the absence of a written agreement between the Parties otherwise.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 3.4 requires Petro-Lube to return or destroy *any* remaining sample and verify its destruction. The changed text in Section 3.4 allows Petro-Lube to retain a portion of the sample for internal purposes and removes the verification requirement. This directly contradicts Section 4.1, which implies confidentiality of information. By retaining a portion of the sample, Petro-Lube could potentially extract confidential information, creating a conflict with the implicit confidentiality of technical or commercial data outlined in Section 4.1. The fact that they can retain a sample without verification, in contrast to needing to return or destroy all of the sample in the original text, makes this a strong contradiction. The definition of Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction is applicable as the obligations are defined inconsistently.

perturbation.original_text: 6.3) Subject in any event to Clause 6.2, the Parties in this Agreement shall not be liable to each other, whether in contract, tort, (including negligence) or otherwise, under this Agreement for any loss of business, loss of products, loss of anticipated or actual profit, loss of goodwill or reputation, loss caused by business interruption or (without prejudice to any heads of loss expressly set out in Clause 6.3) any indirect, special or consequential cost, expense, loss or damage, even if such cost, expense, loss or damage was reasonably foreseeable or might have reasonably been contemplated by either Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.3) Subject in any event to Clause 6.2, the Parties in this Agreement shall not be liable to each other, whether in contract, tort, (including negligence) or otherwise, under this Agreement for any loss of business, loss of products, loss of anticipated or actual profit *only if* such losses are deemed not reasonably foreseeable. All other limitations of liability in this section will still apply.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause 6.3 is designed to exclude all loses described regardless of foreseeability. The modified version changes this by stating that the exclusion of liability for loss of business, products, profit, etc. only applies *if* the losses were not reasonably foreseeable. This creates an in-text contradiction because the 'even if' phrase originally intended to strengthen the liability exclusion in the original section 6.3 is now undermined.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.3) Subject in any event to Clause 6.2, the Parties in this Agreement shall not be liable to each other, whether in contract, tort, (including negligence) or otherwise, under this Agreement for any loss of business, loss of products, loss of anticipated or actual profit *only if* such losses are deemed not reasonably foreseeable. All other limitations of liability in this section will still apply.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies section 6.3, limiting the exclusion of liability for loss of business, products, and profit only to cases where such losses are deemed not reasonably foreseeable. This directly contradicts the original intent of the clause, which was to exclude these liabilities regardless of foreseeability. The contradiction arises because the 'even if' phrase in the original section 6.3, intended to strengthen the liability exclusion, is undermined by the modified text, creating a strong and clear inconsistency within the contract.

perturbation.original_text: 7.4) The Evaluation and supply of Samples will commence as soon as is practicable after the Effective Date. This Agreement will last for one year from the Effective Date. The obligations under this agreement shall continue to remain in force for a period of five (5) years from the Effective Date. This Agreement may be terminated by either Party at any time by giving thirty (30) days prior written notification. This Agreement may be extended by mutual agreement and written consent of both Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: 7.4) The Evaluation and supply of Samples will commence as soon as is practicable after the Effective Date. This Agreement will last for one year from the Effective Date. The obligations under this agreement shall continue to remain in force for a period of five (5) years from the Effective Date, *except confidentiality obligations which will only last for two years*. This Agreement may be terminated by either Party at any time by giving thirty (30) days prior written notification. This Agreement may be extended by mutual agreement and written consent of both Parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause 7.4 states that obligations remain in force for five years from the Effective Date. The inserted phrase shortens the duration of confidentiality obligations to two years. This directly contradicts clause 7.8 which says the existence, terms, and subject matter of this Agreement are confidential *without* mentioning any specific limit to it's confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.8) The existence, terms, and subject matter of this Agreement are confidential between the Parties and are not to be disclosed to any third parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in clause 7.4 introduces an exception to the five-year obligation period for confidentiality, limiting it to two years. This directly contradicts clause 7.8, which states that the confidentiality of the agreement's terms and subject matter is indefinite, with no specified time limit. This creates a clear and direct conflict, making the contract ambiguous and potentially unenforceable regarding the duration of confidentiality obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 8.1) This Agreement, and the relationship between (Your Company Name) and Petro- Lubricant Testing Laboratories, Inc. under this Agreement, shall in all respects be interpreted in accordance with and governed by the laws of the United States of America and the State of (Your State), and the Parties agree to submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of those courts in relation to any dispute arising out of or in connection with this Agreement (whether based in contract, tort (including negligence) or otherwise).
perturbation.changed_text: 8.1) This Agreement, and the relationship between (Your Company Name) and Petro- Lubricant Testing Laboratories, Inc. under this Agreement, shall in all respects be interpreted in accordance with and governed by the laws of the United States of America, *unless otherwise agreed in writing*. The Parties agree to submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of (Your State) in relation to any dispute arising out of or in connection with this Agreement (whether based in contract, tort (including negligence) or otherwise).
perturbation.explanation: Clause 8.1 originally states the agreement is governed by the laws of the United States of America and the State of (Your State). By adding 'unless otherwise agreed in writing', the agreement now allows the parties to potentially choose different governing laws on a case-by-case basis. This creates a conflict with clause 7.6, which states that the agreement constitutes the *whole* agreement between the parties and supersedes all prior agreements.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.6) This Agreement constitutes the whole agreement between the Parties relating to its subject matter and supersedes all prior oral or written agreements, drafts, understandings, undertakings, representations or arrangements relating to the subject matter of this Agreement, except in the case of fraud.
perturbation.justification: The original text of clause 8.1 states the agreement is governed by the laws of the United States of America and the State of (Your State). The changed text adds 'unless otherwise agreed in writing,' which allows parties to choose different governing laws, potentially contradicting clause 7.6. Clause 7.6 states that the agreement constitutes the *whole* agreement between the parties and supersedes all prior agreements, which would prevent the parties from agreeing to other terms not present in the agreement. Therefore, the change introduces an ambiguity that leads to a contradiction with clause 7.6.

perturbation.original_text: 3.5) Petro-Lube agrees to provide a test report to (Your Company Name) of the results of the Evaluations in a form that does not disclose information which Petro-Lube deems to constitute confidential information regarding (Your Company Name), Petro-Lube, or any other third party.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.5) Petro-Lube agrees to provide a test report to (Your Company Name) of the results of the Evaluations. *Petro-Lube has the right to decide the format of the report that does not include information to the extent that it deems necessary for its own interests*.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause mandates that the test report provided by Petro-Lube must not include any confidential information of any party. The edited text introduces the ambiguity that Petro-Lube does not have to include information of any party in report that does not serve its interests. This contradicts clause 3.2.3 stating that Petro-Lube will not use the samples for anything other than directed by Your Company Name and clause 3.3 stating Petro-Lube assures the confidential nature of samples to it's employees.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.2.3) Not to use any Sample for any purpose other than to perform those Evaluations as directed by (Your Company Name).
perturbation.justification: The original text in clause 3.5 ensures that any confidential information of any party is not disclosed in the test report. The changed text allows Petro-Lube to decide the format of the report and potentially exclude information based on its own interests. This contradicts clause 3.2.3, which states that Petro-Lube shall not use samples for purposes other than directed by Your Company Name. The changed text allows Petro-Lube to potentially withhold information, thus implicitly using the sample data/results in a way not directed by Your Company Name to serve Petro-Lube's own interests. This creates a clear conflict, making enforcement and obligations uncertain.
